message:
fix(combinator): poll all futures before returning in SelectAll and SelectAllDrain

SelectAll::poll previously returned immediately upon finding the first
ready future, which meant other simultaneously-ready futures were not
polled in that waker cycle. This could cause subtle issues:
- Wakers for other ready futures might not be registered, leading to
  missed completions on subsequent polls
- In the SelectAllDrain variant, simultaneously-completed futures would
  be incorrectly returned in the losers vec even though they had already
  resolved, causing callers to poll completed futures

Now both combinators poll every future exactly once per poll() call:

- SelectAll: Tracks the first ready future as the winner but continues
  polling remaining futures. Returns the winner after all futures have
  been polled, ensuring all wakers are properly registered.

- SelectAllDrain: Tracks all simultaneously-ready futures. The winner
  is the first one found; any other completed futures are excluded from
  the losers vec via sorted index filtering with binary search. This
  prevents callers from receiving already-resolved futures as "losers"
  that would panic or return stale results when polled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
